objective: In tests/test_cli_doctor.py, change @patch('glitchlab.cli.AGENT_REGISTRY', ...) to @patch('glitchlab.registry.AGENT_REGISTRY', ...) and @patch('glitchlab.cli.STEP_HANDLERS', ...) to @patch('glitchlab.step_handlers.STEP_HANDLERS', ...) in both test functions. Only touch tests/test_cli_doctor.py. Verify: python -m pytest tests/test_cli_doctor.py -v

plan_steps.description: Update the two test functions in tests/test_cli_doctor.py so their patch decorators target the source modules that own the registries, replacing any patch target of glitchlab.cli.AGENT_REGISTRY with glitchlab.registry.AGENT_REGISTRY and glitchlab.cli.STEP_HANDLERS with glitchlab.step_handlers.STEP_HANDLERS, without altering test behavior or any other assertions.

commit_message: test: fix doctor patch targets to source registries
implementation_summary: Updated tests/test_cli_doctor.py so both doctor tests patch AGENT_REGISTRY from glitchlab.registry and STEP_HANDLERS from glitchlab.step_handlers, touching no other files. Verified with python -m pytest tests/test_cli_doctor.py -v (2 passed).
changelog_entry:
### Fixed
- Updated tests/test_cli_doctor.py to patch AGENT_REGISTRY and STEP_HANDLERS from their new module locations in glitchlab.registry and glitchlab.step_handlers respectively. This change affects only tests and does not impact the public API.

data.description: Verifies the doctor CLI command reads AGENT_REGISTRY from glitchlab.registry and STEP_HANDLERS from glitchlab.step_handlers by patching those public module locations and asserting the command output reflects the patched values.
